data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Auto Stocks Decline as Major Brands Experience Losses
data.description: Hong Kong's automotive sector witnessed a downturn with several major brands experiencing significant losses. According to Jin10, Leapmotor (09863.HK) and Geely Automobile Holdings (00175.HK) both saw their shares drop by over 5%. Additionally, XPeng Inc. (09868.HK) shares fell nearly 5%, while BYD Company Limited (01211.HK) and Lantu Motors (07489.HK) experienced declines of over 3%. The market sentiment reflects broader challenges within the automotive industry in Hong Kong.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Challenges in Crypto VC and Opportunities in AI
data.description:
Liquid Capital founder Jack Yi recently shared insights on the X platform regarding the challenges faced by crypto venture capital and projects. According to ChainCatcher, Yi highlighted that a significant issue has been the allocation of funds towards developing web3 products that mirror web2, which he argues is unnecessary since web3 is fundamentally a financial industry. He noted that the most successful companies in the crypto space have been financial products, such as stablecoins, exchanges, and payment systems.

Yi also pointed out the emerging opportunities in the AI era, suggesting that large-scale funding and extensive teams are no longer essential. He believes that AI combined with finance presents new opportunities, and that talented founders with a small team of experts can build top-tier companies. This, he asserts, represents the greatest opportunity in primary investment today.

data.title: Analyst Reports on Bitcoin Trading Activity
data.description: Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that user @Jason60704294 has closed a profitable Bitcoin position. The $75,731.7 BTC long position shared yesterday did not execute, as the market rose shortly after the post without reaching the specified price point. The term 'closed' likely refers to subsequent trades or other executed positions, though the exact profit and position size remain undisclosed.

data.title: Indonesia Secures ¥172.1 Billion in Largest Samurai Bond Sale in Two Years
data.description: Indonesia has successfully raised ¥172.1 billion ($1.1 billion) through its largest Samurai bond sale in two years, driven by robust investor demand. Bloomberg posted on X that the strong interest in Indonesia's credit has surpassed concerns regarding budgetary pressures stemming from the ongoing Middle East conflict. The bond sale highlights Indonesia's ability to attract investment despite geopolitical tensions, reflecting confidence in its economic stability and fiscal management.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks: Zhipu Surges Over 4% to Record High
data.description: Zhipu (02513.HK) experienced a significant increase in its stock price, rising over 4% in Hong Kong trading. According to Jin10, the stock reached a peak of 1045 HKD per share, marking a new historical high.

data.title: South Korean Employee Sentenced for Embezzling Funds for Cryptocurrency Investment
data.description: A South Korean financial employee in their 20s has been sentenced to three years in prison for embezzling company funds to invest in cryptocurrency. According to ChainCatcher, the Busan District Court found the individual guilty of transferring company money into a personal account approximately 680 times between 2021 and 2025. The total amount embezzled was around 570 million Korean won, which was used for cryptocurrency trading, overseas travel, and personal expenses. The employee also forged company deposit trust balance certificates to conceal the crime.

data.title: US stocks closed with a strong rally in the crypto sector, with Strategy (MSTR) rising over 9%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.69%, the S&P 500 rose 1.05%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 1.64%. The crypto sector saw broad gains, with Strategy (MSTR) rising 9.39%, Circle rising 8.69%, Bitmine rising 7.37%, Coinbase rising 5.25%, Solana rising 4.50%, and SharpLink rising 1.49%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Tokenized U.S. Treasurys Market Cap Surpasses $14 Billion
data.description: Tokenized U.S. Treasurys have achieved a record market capitalization exceeding $14 billion. According to NS3.AI, this milestone is based on data from Token Terminal, as reported by Cryptopolitan. A significant contributor to this growth is Franklin Templeton's BENJI on-chain money market fund, which has expanded by over 381% in the past month.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ether Show Strong Momentum Amid Global Economic Concerns
data.description:
The U.S. government's bailout plans and currency swap lines with the United Arab Emirates are alleviating global liquidity fears and reducing credit crisis risks. According to Cointelegraph, record inflows into Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and increasing profits for Bitcoin miners indicate robust bullish momentum, despite the ongoing conflict in Iran.

On Wednesday, the total cryptocurrency market capitalization reached an 11-week high as Bitcoin surged to $79,000 and Ether climbed to $2,400. This bullish trend emerged as investors gained confidence that immediate U.S. recession risks were diminishing, despite the persistent high oil prices due to the war in Iran. Traders are now considering whether Bitcoin and Ether will continue to rise or if a short-term correction is likely, given the ongoing economic recession risks.

The Nasdaq-100 index, known for its tech-heavy composition, hit a record high on Wednesday as traders anticipated Tesla's quarterly earnings. Meanwhile, Brent crude prices increased by 9% over two days following reports of Iran targeting two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Elevated energy costs are increasing the likelihood of economic stimulus, providing a temporary buffer for risk assets.

U.S. President Donald Trump reportedly mentioned in a CNBC interview that the federal government should assist Spirit Airlines, a budget carrier that has faced bankruptcy twice since 2025. The Trump administration has previously provided capital to companies like Intel, Southern Company, and L3Harris. Direct intervention by the U.S. government in private firms and the U.S. Treasury indicates that credit lines for allies have eased liquidity concerns. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent noted that both the U.S. and the UAE would benefit from a currency swap line intended to maintain order in the dollar funding markets.

U.S. allies are under pressure to sell U.S. bonds to raise dollars for local defense, imports, and liquidity amid the collapse of oil revenue and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Potential currency swaps ease these dollar shortages, preventing a spike in U.S. Treasury yields, which results in lower borrowing costs and a reduced risk of an immediate credit crisis.

Six consecutive days of inflows into U.S.-listed Bitcoin ETFs, totaling $1.54 billion, have likely boosted sentiment. The successful launch of the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust, which reached $145 million in total net assets in under three weeks, has improved Bitcoin's risk perception despite global socio-economic uncertainty.

As Bitcoin's price approached $79,000, miner profitability reached its highest level since January, according to Luxor's Hashprice Index. Miners have recently gained attention as firms sold significant Bitcoin holdings to fund investments in data centers and AI infrastructure. While higher profitability does not guarantee reduced selling pressure from miners, the bullish momentum creates an incentive to accumulate.

Ultimately, a short-term correlation with U.S. stock markets continues to influence cryptocurrency trends; therefore, the war in Iran and tech earnings remain decisive for trader sentiment. As the U.S. government signals that stimulus measures will be used to secure liquidity and address credit concerns, Bitcoin and Ether appear primed to sustain their upward momentum.

data.title: Aave TVL fell below $30 billion, with $16.2 billion flowing out from its previous high.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Aave TVL has fallen below $30 billion. This figure has dropped from $45.8 billion before the rsETH incident to the current $29.6 billion, representing an outflow of $16.2 billion.

data.title: Aave's Total Value Locked Falls Below $30 Billion
data.description: Aave's total value locked (TVL) has dropped below $30 billion, according to blockchain analyst Yu Jin. The data indicates a decline from $45.8 billion before the rsETH incident to the current $29.6 billion, marking an outflow of $16.2 billion.

data.title: Aave's Total Deposits Fall Below $30 Billion Amid Recent Events
data.description: Aave's total deposits have decreased significantly, dropping below $30 billion. According to PANews, on-chain analyst Yu Jin reported that the deposits fell from $45.8 billion to $29.6 billion, marking an outflow of $16.2 billion following the rsETH incident.

data.title: Websea and Luffa have entered into a deep partnership to jointly explore new models for the integration of Web3 social and transaction ecosystems.
data.description: Digital asset trading platform Websea and Web3 creator and fan economy ecosystem application Luffa have officially entered into a deep partnership. The two companies will collaborate extensively across multiple dimensions, including traffic sharing, content co-creation, expansion of trading scenarios, and enhancement of commercial value, jointly exploring new models for the integration of Web3 social networks and digital asset trading. Websea will leverage Luffa's existing user base to gradually convert potential users into digital asset trading users; simultaneously, Websea's channel network will bring more new registered users to Luffa. At the product and feature level, Websea will also deeply integrate with Luffa to further expand trading scenarios.

data.title: Wallets associated with the Worldcoin team/investors deposited 4.63 million WLD tokens into Binance, resulting in a paper loss of $8.53 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet (0xDe2...fF2c) associated with the Worldcoin team/investors deposited 4.63 million WLD tokens, worth $1.25 million, into Binance. This address received these WLD tokens for $9.78 million a year ago and has now lost $8.53 million.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines as Bitcoin Outperforms
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index has decreased by three points, reaching a level of 34. According to NS3.AI, this index measures the performance of the top 100 cryptocurrencies, excluding stablecoins and wrapped tokens, in comparison to Bitcoin over the last 90 days.

data.title: Solana App Founder Ben Pasternak Arrested on Assault Charges
data.description: Ben Pasternak, founder of the Solana ecosystem application Believe, has been arrested and charged with second-degree strangulation and third-degree assault, according to New York Criminal Court records. According to Foresight News, previous reports from SolanaFloor, citing the Australian Financial Review, indicate that Pasternak is facing charges in a New York court related to his alleged involvement in a crypto rug pull scheme, which resulted in millions of dollars in losses.

data.title: Believe founder arrested, faces second-degree murder and assault charges.
data.description: According to public records from the New York Criminal Court, Benjamin Pasternak, founder of Believe, has been arrested on charges of second-degree murder and assault and is scheduled to appear in court on June 11. He was previously accused of participating in a multi-million dollar token absconding scheme. The platform's native token, BELIEVE, is currently trading at approximately $0.00075, a drop of over 99% from its all-time high of $0.3569 in May 2025.
